text:
Martha loved fox hunts. She woke up one chilly Saturday morning especially excited.

She had invited friends over to hear fox tales. They'd roast Chestnuts, drink cider, and talk owl chatter.

First, she'd fill up the old hunter boots. Martha sewn her own patterns. They were cozy and red.

Then, she took her falconry book. Owl calling seemed quaint tonight.

They hunted together! Wait till dusk. Owls swooped.

Her fox did great! Martha called him big feather.

They feasted! Moonlight on mashed potatoes.

Friends laughed. Martha thought they dreamt.

Still, foxes felt real.

Come November. They'd listen.

She'd think.

Just wait.

Her garden grew.

The fox loved burrows.

Now they shared.

Winter quiet.

Stars fell like jewels.

She'd hear him.

She'd think.

Come spring.

Just think.

Maybe next.

So they'd go.

She saw him sometimes now.

I'll bet.<|endoftext|>
